growth_records.strain: isolate from shallow-water hydrothermal vent (16S rRNA >99.7% S. rubidaea)

up.units: not quantified at full-strength MB; dissolved O2 depletion used only as growth proxy
up.method: integrated fluorescence dissolved-O2 sensor spot in 24-well plate

sec.units: mg/L (final titer, not a per-time rate)

yields.note: MB gave 0.62 mg/L prodigiosin; among highest-titer media tested.

growth_records.cite: Pereira, Ricardo F. S. et al. Isolation and Characterization of a Serratia rubidaea from a Shallow Water Hydrothermal Vent. Marine Drugs (2023). DOI:10.3390/md21120599. [PMC10745058]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively (fastest growth at 30 and 37 C; no numeric mu given)
growth_records.snippet: The studied S. rubidaea strains grew faster in MB at 30 and 37 C than at 15 and 62 C... Both marine-based media, F2S and MB, allowed the highest product concentrations, which reached 0.66 and 0.62 mg/L, respectively
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (Enterobacteriaceae, marine isolate). MB, 30 C, 150 rpm, aerobic shaken flask. No numeric specific growth rate reported (only qualitative 'fastest at 30/37 C'). Prodigiosin is a SECRETED secondary metabolite: 0.62 mg/L final titer in MB (NOT a per-time rate; cannot compute q without time-course numbers). MB is commercial Marine Broth (Condalab) - composition not itemized in paper. O2 depletion measured only as growth proxy, not quantified as an uptake rate at full-strength MB. Nutrient-dilution study (diluted MB) gave specific O2 consumption 10.4% DO/h at 1:15 dilution and 7.4% DO/h at 1:40 dilution.

growth_records.strain: isolate from shallow-water hydrothermal vent (16S rRNA >99.7% S. rubidaea)

yields.note: Biomass 0.85 g/L attained in first 1.8 h at 62 C; growth then ceased. Pigment production significantly decreased at 62 C but strain still produces prodigiosin up to 62 C (unlike S. marcescens, capped at 36-37 C).

growth_records.cite: Pereira, Ricardo F. S. et al. Isolation and Characterization of a Serratia rubidaea from a Shallow Water Hydrothermal Vent. Marine Drugs (2023). DOI:10.3390/md21120599. [PMC10745058]
growth_records.method: reported (biomass reached 0.85 g/L within first 1.8 h then no further increase); no mu computed (initial biomass not given)
growth_records.snippet: cells growing at 62 C attained a concentration of 0.85 g/L in the first 1.8 h, but no additional increase in cell number was observed... At 37 and 62 C, S. rubidaea cells significantly decreased the production of the red product
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote, thermotolerant marine isolate. MB, 62 C, aerobic shaken flask. Measured biomass titer 0.85 g/L reached in 1.8 h (REPORTED). mu NOT computable: no initial biomass / no exponential OD slope reported. Prodigiosin secretion strongly decreased at 62 C but non-zero; strain uniquely produces pigment up to 62 C. Highest-temperature growth condition documented.

growth_records.strain: isolate from shallow-water hydrothermal vent (16S rRNA >99.7% S. rubidaea)
growth_records.medium: F2S (F2 seawater medium + 5 g/L meat peptone + 1 g/L yeast extract)

sec.units: mg/L (final titer, highest across media tested)

yields.note: F2S gave the highest prodigiosin titer (0.66 mg/L) but contains thermolabile vitamins; MB (0.62 mg/L) chosen for scale-up.

growth_records.cite: Pereira, Ricardo F. S. et al. Isolation and Characterization of a Serratia rubidaea from a Shallow Water Hydrothermal Vent. Marine Drugs (2023). DOI:10.3390/md21120599. [PMC10745058]
growth_records.snippet: Both marine-based media, F2S and MB, allowed the highest product concentrations, which reached 0.66 and 0.62 mg/L, respectively
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote. F2S medium (F2 seawater base + 5 g/L meat peptone + 1 g/L yeast extract), 30 C, 150 rpm, aerobic. Prodigiosin titer 0.66 mg/L (REPORTED, highest of all media screened). Full F2 base composition not itemized in paper (marine algae/diatom seawater medium). Titer, not a per-time rate.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 5159 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/5159
